heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Stanley Kubrick. Written by Dalton Trumbo, based on the novel of the same name by Howard Fast.
clean: Directed by Stanley Kubrick. Written by Dalton Trumbo, based on the novel of the same name by Howard Fast.

heading: Spartacus
raw: I'd rather be here, a free man among brothers, facing a long march and a hard fight, than the richest citizen in Rome: fat with food he didn't work for, and surrounded by slaves.
clean: I'd rather be here, a free man among brothers, facing a long march and a hard fight, than the richest citizen in Rome: fat with food he didn't work for, and surrounded by slaves.

heading: Spartacus
raw: When a free man dies, he loses the pleasure of life. A slave loses his pain. Death is the only freedom a slave knows. That's why he's not afraid of it. That's why we'll win.
clean: When a free man dies, he loses the pleasure of life. A slave loses his pain. Death is the only freedom a slave knows. That's why he's not afraid of it. That's why we'll win.

heading: Spartacus
raw: I am Spartacus!
clean: I am Spartacus!

heading: Spartacus
raw: Crixus always wanted to march on Rome. Now he doesn't have to. Rome has come to us.
clean: Crixus always wanted to march on Rome. Now he doesn't have to. Rome has come to us.

heading: Spartacus
raw: He'll come back. He'll come back, and he'll be millions!
clean: He'll come back. He'll come back, and he'll be millions!

heading: Batiatus
raw: Good luck, and may fortune smile upon... most of you.
clean: Good luck, and may fortune smile upon... most of you.

heading: Batiatus
raw: But I'm a civilian. I'm more of a civilian than most civilians.
clean: But I'm a civilian. I'm more of a civilian than most civilians.

heading: Marcus Licinius Crassus
raw: If there was no Rome, I'd dream of her. If there were no gods, I'd revere them.
clean: If there was no Rome, I'd dream of her. If there were no gods, I'd revere them.

heading: Marcus Licinius Crassus
raw: I'm not after glory, I'm after Spartacus! And gentlemen, I mean to have him. However, this campaign is not about killing Spartacus. It is to kill the legend of Spartacus.
clean: I'm not after glory, I'm after Spartacus! And gentlemen, I mean to have him. However, this campaign is not about killing Spartacus. It is to kill the legend of Spartacus.

heading: Marcus Licinius Crassus
raw: One of the disadvantages of being a patrician is that occasionally you're obliged to act like one.
clean: One of the disadvantages of being a patrician is that occasionally you're obliged to act like one.

heading: Gracchus
raw: You know, this republic of ours is something like a rich widow. Most Romans love her as their mother, but Crassus dreams of marrying the old girl, to put it politely.
clean: You know, this republic of ours is something like a rich widow. Most Romans love her as their mother, but Crassus dreams of marrying the old girl, to put it politely.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: What is your name?
clean: What is your name?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Draba: You don't want to know mine. I don't want to know your name.
clean: You don't want to know mine. I don't want to know your name.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Just a friendly question.
clean: Just a friendly question.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Draba: Gladiators don't make friends. If we're ever matched in the arena together, I have to kill you.
clean: Gladiators don't make friends. If we're ever matched in the arena together, I have to kill you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: [watching two Roman nobles being forced by a slave on horseback to fight to the death] Ah-ha ha! Come on, fat boy! Yeah!
clean: Ah-ha ha! Come on, fat boy! Yeah!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Slaves: [as Spartacus enters the arena] Spartacus! Hey, Spartacus!
clean: Spartacus! Hey, Spartacus!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Noble Romans, fight each other like animals. [gestures to the slaves on the balcony] Your new masters, betting to see who'll die first. [the slaves laugh] Drop your weapons. [the slaves start booing]
clean: Noble Romans, fight each other like animals. Your new masters, betting to see who'll die first. Drop your weapons.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Slaves: No! No! No! No!
clean: No! No! No! No!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crixus: I want to see their blood, right here where Draba died! [jumps down and draws his sword] When I fight matched pairs, they fight to the death!
clean: I want to see their blood, right here where Draba died! When I fight matched pairs, they fight to the death!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: When you got wine, you don't need bread!
clean: When you got wine, you don't need bread!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: You can't just be a gang of drunken raiders.
clean: You can't just be a gang of drunken raiders.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: What else can we be?
clean: What else can we be?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Gladiators, an army of gladiators. There's never been an army like that. One gladiator is worth any two Roman soldiers that ever lived.
clean: Gladiators, an army of gladiators. There's never been an army like that. One gladiator is worth any two Roman soldiers that ever lived.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crixus: We beat the Romans guards here, but a Roman army is different. They fight different than we do, too.
clean: We beat the Romans guards here, but a Roman army is different. They fight different than we do, too.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: We can beat anything they send against us if we really want to.
clean: We can beat anything they send against us if we really want to.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crixus: It takes a big army.
clean: It takes a big army.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: We'll have a big army. Once we're on the march, we'll free every slave in every town and village. Can anybody get a bigger army than that?
clean: We'll have a big army. Once we're on the march, we'll free every slave in every town and village. Can anybody get a bigger army than that?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: That's right. Once we cross the Alps, we're safe.
clean: That's right. Once we cross the Alps, we're safe.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crixus: Nobody can cross the Alps. Every pass is defended by its own legion.
clean: Nobody can cross the Alps. Every pass is defended by its own legion.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Stand up. On your feet. Stand up, the way a noble Roman should.
clean: Stand up. On your feet. Stand up, the way a noble Roman should.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Slave: That's Roman pride for you, Spartacus! [the slaves laugh]
clean: That's Roman pride for you, Spartacus!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: That's better. What's your name?
clean: That's better. What's your name?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Glabrus: Marcus, Glabrus.
clean: Marcus, Glabrus.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Glabrus.
clean: Glabrus.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Glabrus: Commander of the Garrison of Rome!
clean: Commander of the Garrison of Rome!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: Commander?
clean: Commander?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crixus: He was commanding it on his belly when we found him, playing dead! [the slaves laugh]
clean: He was commanding it on his belly when we found him, playing dead!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: You disappoint me, Marcus Glabrus, playing dead. You afraid to die? It's easy to die. Haven't you seen enough gladiators in the arena to see how easy it is to die?
clean: You disappoint me, Marcus Glabrus, playing dead. You afraid to die? It's easy to die. Haven't you seen enough gladiators in the arena to see how easy it is to die?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Glabrus: Why...what are you going to do to me?
clean: Why...what are you going to do to me?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: I don't know. [turns to the slaves] What should we do with him?
clean: I don't know. What should we do with him?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: Let's have a matched pair, him and me! [the slaves laugh]
clean: Let's have a matched pair, him and me!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Glabrus: I'll not fight like a gladiator!
clean: I'll not fight like a gladiator!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: [showing Glabrus a Roman baton] You keep staring at this. You recognize this baton?
clean: You keep staring at this. You recognize this baton?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: You should! It was in your tent. [holds up the baton] The symbol of the Senate! All the power of Rome! [grips and snaps the baton in two]
clean: You should! It was in your tent. The symbol of the Senate! All the power of Rome!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dionysus: That's the power of Rome!
clean: That's the power of Rome!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Spartacus: [thrusting the broken baton at Glabrus] Take that back to your senate. Tell them you and that broken stick is all that's left of the garrison of Rome! Tell them we want nothing from Rome, nothing, except our freedom!
clean: Take that back to your senate. Tell them you and that broken stick is all that's left of the garrison of Rome! Tell them we want nothing from Rome, nothing, except our freedom!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Do you steal?
clean: Do you steal?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: No, master.
clean: No, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Do you lie?
clean: Do you lie?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: Not if I can avoid it.
clean: Not if I can avoid it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus': Have you... ever dishonored the gods?
clean: Have you... ever dishonored the gods?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: No, master.
clean: No, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus': Do you refrain from these vices out of respect for moral virtues?
clean: Do you refrain from these vices out of respect for moral virtues?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: Yes, master.
clean: Yes, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Do you eat oysters?
clean: Do you eat oysters?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: When I have them, master.
clean: When I have them, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Do you eat snails?
clean: Do you eat snails?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: No, master.
clean: No, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Do you consider the eating of oysters to be moral, and the eating of snails to be immoral?
clean: Do you consider the eating of oysters to be moral, and the eating of snails to be immoral?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: No, master.
clean: No, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: Of course not. It is all a matter of taste, isn't it?
clean: Of course not. It is all a matter of taste, isn't it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: Yes, master.
clean: Yes, master.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Crassus: And taste is not the same as appetite, and therefore not a question of morals, hmm?
clean: And taste is not the same as appetite, and therefore not a question of morals, hmm?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoninus: It could be argued so, master.
clean: It could be argued so, master.
